overview: Add `c8volt get cluster license` under the existing `get cluster` hierarchy, reuse the current cluster license service and JSON output pattern, and finish with focused command tests plus synchronized documentation and CLI doc updates.

userStories.title: Establish the cluster license command entry point
userStories.description: Add the base `get cluster license` Cobra command and shared handler in the existing `cmd/` structure so the new feature has a repository-native entry point before behavior and docs are expanded.
acceptanceCriteria: A `license` command exists beneath `get cluster` in the Cobra tree.
The new command is wired in `cmd/` without introducing a parallel or legacy direct `cluster-license` command path.
The command entry point reuses existing CLI construction patterns already used by neighboring cluster read commands.
userStories.notes: Keep this slice limited to command registration and handler setup in `cmd/get_cluster_license.go` using existing repository patterns.

userStories.title: Deliver successful cluster license retrieval
userStories.description: Implement the success path for `c8volt get cluster license` by calling the existing cluster service and printing the returned `domain.License` payload through the standard JSON helper.
acceptanceCriteria: Running `c8volt get cluster license` with a valid config returns the structured license payload successfully.
Optional license fields remain absent when the service does not provide them instead of being replaced with invented values.
Command tests cover the successful execution path with an explicit temp config.
userStories.notes: Route directly to `cli.GetClusterLicense(cmd.Context())`. Keep the implementation bounded to the CLI command and reuse the existing service/domain model.

userStories.title: Lock down help discovery and failure semantics
userStories.description: Make the new command discoverable through `get` help surfaces and prove that failure handling matches existing `get` command behavior, including exit-code assertions through subprocess-based tests where needed.
acceptanceCriteria: `c8volt get` and `c8volt get cluster` help expose `license` as a supported subcommand.
A failing `c8volt get cluster license` execution yields the established CLI failure semantics and exit code.
Command tests cover help discovery and failing execution behavior.
userStories.notes: Follow the repository testing guidance in `cmd/get_test.go`: reset Cobra flags between in-process runs and use subprocess helpers when `ferrors.HandleAndExit` terminates via `os.Exit`.

userStories.title: Align documentation and complete validation
userStories.description: Update the source documentation surfaces that describe cluster read commands, regenerate the CLI reference pages from Cobra metadata, and run the targeted plus repository-wide validation flow.
acceptanceCriteria: README and `docs/index.md` mention `c8volt get cluster license` wherever cluster read command discovery is documented.
Generated CLI docs include the new cluster license command and are refreshed from source help text rather than hand-edited.
Validation includes `go test ./cmd/... -race -count=1`, `go test ./internal/services/cluster/... -race -count=1`, `make docs`, and `make test`.
userStories.notes: Update source docs first, then regenerate `docs/cli/`. Do not hand-edit generated output when the repository already provides `make docs`.
